message:
feat(abi): port __cxa_pure_virtual Itanium C++ ABI stub (bd-g1wfq)

Adds the Itanium C++ ABI `__cxa_pure_virtual()` entry point. C++
compilers populate vtable slots for pure virtual functions with a
pointer to this symbol; it gets invoked at runtime if a caller
dispatches through an under-construction or partially-destructed
object whose vtable still points to a pure-virtual stub.

Without this symbol, every C++ binary linked against our libc
fails at link time with an undefined reference to `__cxa_pure_virtual`.

Implementation: a no-return `extern "C"` fn that writes the
glibc-conventional diagnostic ("pure virtual method called\n") to
stderr via `libc::write` and calls `libc::abort()`. Marked `-> !`.

Tests: 1 fork-based abi integration test that calls
__cxa_pure_virtual in the child and verifies the child terminates
via SIGABRT (WIFSIGNALED + WTERMSIG == SIGABRT). The diagnostic
text "pure virtual method called" appears on stderr during the
test as expected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
